pattern_description: MEDIATION (A → M → B): A causes B entirely through a mediator M. There is no direct path from A to B. Tests whether the LLM understands indirect effects and can identify the mediating mechanism rather than treating A and B as directly linked.
difficulty_notes: Easy. 3 nodes, 2 edges. Similar in structure to a chain but framed around a mechanism question — useful for testing whether the LLM can articulate the *why* behind an indirect effect.
description: Exercise improves overall health by reducing body weight. The effect is fully mediated through weight.

explanation_points: Exercise affects health only indirectly, through its effect on body weight
Body Weight is the mediator in this relationship
There is no direct arrow from Exercise to Health in this model
If body weight did not change, exercise would have no effect on health in this model

counterfactual.expected_outcome: Body weight decreases, and as a result, health improves. The effect on health is indirect, flowing through body weight.
